page: Microsoft Azure EC8as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ECasv6 server family. Powered by an AMD processor and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 8 dedicated virtual CPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, positioning it as a memory-optimized resource.

The EC8as_v6 does not include local temporary storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external volumes. It is Premium Storage capable, allowing integration with high-performance persistent disks to handle input/output operations. Additionally, the instance does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it operates without dedicated GPU hardware accelerators.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high resource density of 8 GB of RAM per vCPU allows organizations to run memory-heavy workloads without provisioning excess compute cores. This makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive applications, database caching layers, enterprise resource planning systems, and relational databases that require a larger memory footprint relative to processing power.
description: Microsoft Azure EC8as_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ECasv6 family, featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and managed by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, allowing for external high-performance disk attachments. With no integrated GPUs and zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this server is designed for memory-intensive workloads. The high memory-to-CPU ratio offers a cost-efficient profile for applications requiring substantial memory capacity without the need for excessive compute resources. It is suited for relational databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
